sections.title: Welcome to Your Experience With Cycling
sections.description:
This SmartUrbanity questionnaire focuses on your experience as a cyclist or e-bike rider. Your answers help us understand the conditions and barriers people face when cycling in the city, and identify opportunities to make cycling safer, more accessible, and more practical for everyday travel. Participation is voluntary and anonymous.

https://www.smarturbanity.com/about

sections.title: Cycling Habits
sections.description: Questions about how and when you cycle as part of your daily travel.

questions.text: Do you cycle (including e-bikes) as a mode of travel at least once a week?

options.text: Yes
No

questions.text: Which type of bicycle do you most often use?

options.text: Standard pedal bicycle (private)
Electric bicycle / e-bike (private)
Shared / hire bicycle (docked or dockless)
Shared / hire e-bike
Cargo bike or adapted bike

questions.text: How confident are you cycling in urban traffic in your city?

options.text: Not confident at all — I avoid cycling in traffic
Not very confident — I cycle only when I have no alternative
Neither confident nor unconfident
Somewhat confident — I manage most situations comfortably
Very confident — I cycle in all conditions without difficulty
I have never cycled in urban traffic

questions.text: How adequate is the cycling infrastructure (cycle lanes, paths, and signage) on your typical route?

options.text: Very inadequate — little or no cycling infrastructure exists
Inadequate — some provision exists but it is fragmented or poorly designed
Partially adequate — infrastructure covers part of my route
Mostly adequate — infrastructure is reasonably good along most of my route
Very adequate — cycling infrastructure is excellent throughout my route

questions.text: Which of the following physical barriers do you face on your cycling route? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Uneven or poorly maintained road surfaces (potholes, patches, cobblestones)
Steep or sudden changes in gradient
Parked cars or vehicles blocking cycle lanes
Pedestrians or e-scooters using cycle lanes
Road furniture blocking cycle paths (bins, signage, advertising boards)
Lack of cycle-friendly crossings or traffic signals
Poorly designed junctions or roundabouts
I don't face any physical barrier
Other

questions.text: If you encounter poor road or path surfaces (potholes, cobblestones, or uneven ground), how does it affect your cycling?

options.text: It does not affect my cycling at all
I notice it but continue without changing my behaviour
I slow down or adjust my line but stay on the same route
I actively avoid roads with poor surfaces, even if the alternative is longer
Poor surfaces significantly limit where and how often I can cycle

questions.text: To what extent does the steepness of your route affect your cycling?

options.text: Not at all
Slightly — I notice hills but they do not significantly affect my journey
Moderately — hills slow me down or increase my effort noticeably
Significantly — I avoid steep routes when possible
Extremely — hills are a major barrier that limits where I can cycle

questions.text: How does the quality of signs and wayfinding on your cycling route affect your journey?

options.text: Not at all — I can navigate easily regardless of signage quality
Slightly — signs are occasionally helpful
Moderately — I rely on signs to confirm my route
Significantly — unclear or missing signs sometimes disrupt my journey
Very significantly — poor signage makes navigation difficult without GPS or phone apps

questions.text: How do traffic levels and vehicle speeds on your cycling route typically affect your journey?

options.text: Not at all — traffic does not affect my cycling experience
Slightly — I notice traffic, but it rarely affects my comfort or route
Moderately — traffic sometimes reduces my comfort or enjoyment while cycling
Significantly — traffic often makes me feel stressed or unsafe
Very significantly — I change my route or travel time specifically to avoid heavy or fast traffic

questions.text: Which categories of road users most negatively affect your sense of safety while cycling? (Select up to 3 options)

options.text: Car drivers
Motorcycle or scooter riders
Bus drivers
Delivery vehicles (vans, cargo bikes, scooters)
Taxi or ride-hailing drivers
Other cyclists
E-scooter riders
Pedestrians
None — no category negatively affects my feeling of safety
Other

questions.text: How confident are you in your ability to anticipate and react to dangerous situations while cycling (e.g., car doors opening suddenly, vehicles cutting across, or blind junctions)?

options.text: Not confident at all — I feel unprepared for these situations
Slightly confident — I am aware of the risks but often feel caught off guard
Moderately confident — I manage most situations but some still worry me
Quite confident — I anticipate most hazards and respond well
Very confident — I feel well prepared for all typical cycling hazards

questions.text: How does street lighting affect your decision to cycle after dark?

options.text: Lighting levels have no influence on my decision to cycle after dark
I notice poor lighting but it does not change when or where I cycle
I cycle at reduced speed or stay on higher-lit roads in poorly lit areas
I take significantly longer routes to stay in well-lit areas
I do not cycle after dark because of poor lighting

questions.text: How would you describe the availability of bicycle parking at your most frequent destination?

options.text: Very inadequate — there is nowhere secure to park my bicycle
Inadequate — parking exists but is too limited or too far from my destination
Partially adequate — parking is available but not always convenient or secure
Mostly adequate — I can usually find a suitable place to park
Very adequate — secure and convenient parking is always available

questions.text: Has the lack of secure bicycle parking ever caused you to change your travel plans or leave your bike at home?

options.text: Never
Rarely
Sometimes
Often
Always — lack of parking is a constant barrier to cycling

questions.text: What improvements would most encourage you to cycle more often or for longer distances? (Select up to 3 options)

options.text: More dedicated and physically separated cycle lanes
Better road surface quality on cycling routes
More secure bicycle parking at destinations
More secure bicycle storage near home
Reduced vehicle speeds in residential areas
Better cycle-friendly junctions and crossings
More trees and shade along cycling routes
Better street lighting on cycling routes
Access to cycle training or skills courses
Financial incentives (e.g., employer cycling allowance, subsidised bike purchase)
Nothing — I am satisfied with current conditions
Other
